children.text: Which causes the greatest compass error on a long easterly and westerly cross-country flights?
Which publication contains detailed Canadian airport information?

An aeroplane flew from the Langley airport with an altimeter setting of 29.82 in Hg to the Victoria Harbour water aerodrome at sea level and the altimeter indicates 100 feet. The correct altimeter setting for Victoria Harbour is
Westerly variation is . . . . . a true heading to obtain the magnetic heading.
In the Northern Hemisphere a magnetic compass will normally indicate initially a turn toward the west

answers: when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.
during deceleration while on a southerly heading.
during acceleration while on a northerly heading.
correctAnswer: when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.

children.text: In the Northern Hemisphere a magnetic compass will normally indicate initially a turn toward the east

answers: during acceleration while on a northerly heading.
during deceleration while on a southerly heading.
when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.
correctAnswer: when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
